title: The Subagent Tax. Claude Code Fan-Outs Cost Up to 5.9x the Tokens, and Were Never Faster
excerpt: Claude Code subagents used 2.6x to 5.9x the tokens of working sequentially, and were never faster. We measured the fan-out tax and the fixes.
content_html:
Claude Code's subagents used 2.6x to 5.9x as many input tokens as doing the same work sequentially, measured across three model families. They were not faster on any task we timed. And in one matched pair, cache misses pushed the price-weighted cost to roughly five times the sequential run.

Be the first to know when we publish original research or thought pieces on AI and quantum governance.

No spam. Unsubscribe at any time.

Subagents look like cheap parallelism because the parent receives only their compact results. Underneath, each one is a separate agent loop that carries its own system prompt, tools, and cache entries on every turn it takes.

Since early July, Claude Code's built-in Explore agents also inherit the parent model rather than defaulting to Haiku, so an unnoticed fan-out now runs on whatever expensive model you happen to be driving.

When we published our study of coding-agent token overhead, the loudest comment thread was about exactly this. One reader's task quietly spawned seven subagents that drained a five-hour budget, a review step tried to recruit forty-one verifiers, and later reports reached one hundred and two agents, then four hundred and fifteen. The original study held one measured fan-out already, 513,000 tokens against 121,000 for the same work done directly, its largest single multiplier. Here is the full picture, and the settings that cut the bill.

This is a follow-up to our harness token study. The method, the logging proxy, and the tamper-evident dataset are the same, and the rig is open source.

Why subagents feel free and are not

A subagent looks like delegation. The parent hands off a job, the subagent goes away and works, and a tidy result comes back. The parent's own context stays clean.

The cost hides in three places that the tidy result does not show you.

Each subagent is a fresh agent. It boots with its own system prompt and its own tool schemas, and it re-reads that baseline on every turn it takes, not once.

The parent can pay again on resume. While subagents run, the orchestrator's cached context can age out, and a fan-out that outlasts the cache window re-primes the parent's stack at write rates. Our lanes were short enough that the parent returned to a warm cache every time, so treat this one as mechanism rather than measurement.

And the work multiplies. Two subagents running several turns each add several more full-baseline requests to the session, on top of the parent's own.

What one fan-out actually cost

We gave Claude Code a task and let it fan out to two subagents. We ran the identical task directly for comparison. Both produced correct results.

The direct run took roughly 121,000 cumulative metered input tokens. The fan-out took 513,000, a 4.2x multiplier.

The captured requests show where it went.

Every one of those five subagent requests carried its own 3,554-char system prompt and 24 of the 27 tools, and re-read a 38,000 to 69,000 token prefix. Five separate agent loops, each re-paying its own baseline on every turn, is the tax.

The parent ingests results, not transcripts

One thing the data settles cleanly. The parent does not swallow each subagent's entire transcript.

Across its three requests the parent's message payload grew from 8,551 to 12,490 to 15,332 characters. Those increments are the size of returned results, a few thousand characters each, not whole sub-sessions. A full subagent transcript would be tens of thousands of characters.

So the multiplication is not the parent re-reading everything the subagents did. It is the subagents themselves, each a full agent in its own right, running in parallel.

Are the subagent prompts cached, or re-sent each time

This was the sharpest disagreement in the thread. Some readers said the shared system prompt is cached and therefore cheap. Others said subagents stopped caching it.

Our captures lean towards cached, and the fresh lanes strengthen it across both new model families. In every same-model fan-out lane on Opus and on Fable, the first subagent request read 39,000 to 88,000 tokens from cache and wrote only a few hundred to roughly 6,000. That is the signature of a cache hit on the shared prefix, not a fresh write.

The exceptions matter more, and a thread comment predicted their mechanism. Parallel spawns can race the cache; identical requests fired before the first has finished writing its prefix cannot read it, and there is no in-flight deduplication to save them.

Our captures carry exactly that signature. Every unexpected full-prefix re-write inside a fan-out lane, meaning one where the prefix had already been written or read earlier in the lane, landed while a sibling request of the same profile was still in flight. One Haiku subagent fired six seconds after its sibling and wrote its full 52,022-token prefix with zero cache read. One Fable subagent fired twelve seconds behind two siblings that had each read the same 47,552-token prefix, and missed with a 40,829-token write. One Opus subagent turn re-wrote 56,906 tokens mid-lane with a sibling in flight. We found no unexpected re-write without concurrency.

The counterpoint appeared too. The five-subagent Opus lane fired its five spawns inside twenty seconds against a prefix warmed by an earlier lane, and every one of them read clean. So the shared prompt is cached, and a simultaneous fan-out against a cold cache is exactly where it misses, re-paying the full prefix at premium write rates each time. Caches also do not cross models, so subagents pinned to a different model maintain their own cache entry, which is how a Haiku miss can happen under an Opus parent.

The remaining caveat is unchanged. All of this ran through Meridian, an open-source local proxy that bridges a Claude Max or Pro subscription with standard developer environments by translating requests from external coding applications into the official format required by the Claude Agent SDK, the underlying engine for Claude Code. Meridian manages caching itself, and cache attribution is exactly the measurement a gateway is most likely to distort. The pattern is now consistent across lanes and model families, but a raw-API confirmation stays on the list before we state it as settled fact.

Fan-out versus sequential, measured

We ran the same small task three ways on Opus, through the same rig, each in a fresh workspace isolated from the others. All three completed the task correctly. Wall time in the tables below is the elapsed clock time from launching a lane to its final response.

Two subagents cost 2.6x the sequential run in metered tokens. Five cost 3.2x. On Sonnet in the original study the two-subagent multiplier was 4.2x, so the width of the tax varies with the model and with how many turns each subagent decides to take.

The metered totals understate the price difference. The sequential lane's tokens were mostly cache reads, billed at a tenth of the input rate. The two-subagent lane's cost was concentrated in premium-rate cache writes, including a 49,576-token write on the parent's first turn and that 56,906-token mid-lane re-write. Weight each lane by published API cache pricing and the two-subagent fan-out lands at roughly five times the sequential cost, and the five-subagent lane at roughly three times. Subscription plans meter quota rather than list prices, so the weighting is an API-rate statement, not a claim about your Max allowance.

That ordering surprises people. Five subagents metered more tokens than two but cost less in weighted terms, because the two-subagent lane suffered two full prefix re-writes and the five-subagent lane did not. The mid-session re-write events the original study flagged move the bill around more than fan-out width does.

One fairness note. The lanes ran back to back, so the sequential lane enjoyed a warm cache on its reads. Give it a cold first write and the two-subagent multiplier drops from 2.6x to roughly 2.2x metered. Every conclusion survives the adjustment.

And the speed argument never showed up. The two-subagent lane took eight minutes against the sequential lane's four and a quarter, and even five parallel subagents only matched sequential pace. On a task this small the orchestration overhead swallowed the parallelism whole.

The same matrix on Fable 5

We repeated the three-way matrix on Claude Fable 5, first confirming the gateway was genuinely serving Fable rather than silently substituting another model. All three lanes completed the task correctly.

The tax is wider on Fable than on Opus. The two-subagent fan-out cost 5.9x its sequential baseline, against 2.6x on Opus, largely because Fable's subagents took more turns over the same work.

The inversion shows up again, and this time in the raw metered numbers. Five subagents metered fewer tokens than two, because the two-subagent lane's workers ran four turns each while the five-subagent lane's ran roughly three. How many turns each subagent chooses to take moves the bill more than how many subagents you spawn.

Fable also extends the model-conditional prompt finding from the original study down into subagent traffic. The subagent system prompt measured 3,554 chars on Sonnet, 3,981 on Opus, and 4,509 on Fable, always with the same reduced 24-tool set.

One disclosure applies to every Fable row. The gateway served a few late parent turns as Opus with no fallback marker, one request in the sequential lane, three in the two-subagent lane, and one in the five-subagent lane, while every subagent request was genuinely served Fable. All three lanes share that mixture, so the multipliers between them stand; treat the absolute Fable figures as gateway-mixed.

The model-inheritance trap

Since Claude Code 2.1.198, released on 1 July 2026, the built-in Explore agent inherits the main session's model, capped at Opus, instead of running on Haiku as it used to. (changelog)

That is a quiet cost change. A fan-out that used to spin up cheap Haiku explorers now spins up copies of whatever expensive model you are driving, unless you say otherwise.

We measured what saying otherwise is worth. The same two-subagent fan-out ran once with inherited Opus subagents and once with the subagents pinned to Haiku while the parent stayed on Opus.

The pinned lane metered 37 per cent fewer input tokens, finished in under half the time, and moved roughly 150,000 of its remaining tokens onto Haiku's price sheet, a small fraction of Opus rates. The Haiku subagents also finished in three requests where the Opus ones took eight, which is its own saving.

One new cost appears. Caches do not cross models, so the Haiku subagents maintain their own cache entry, and one of ours re-wrote its full 52,022-token prefix with zero cache read. On a one-off fan-out that write eats into the saving; across a session of repeated fan-outs it amortises.

This is one matched pair, not a distribution. But the direction is unambiguous, and it prices the changelog change above. An Explore swarm that inherits Opus instead of running on Haiku costs roughly the difference between those two rows every time it fires.

How to cut the tax

The thread supplied the mitigations. Here is what we can put numbers on.

Pin subagents to a cheaper model rather than inheriting the driver. Measured above; 37 per cent fewer metered tokens, under half the wall time, and the subagent traffic billed at Haiku rates.

Prefer sequential work when the sub-tasks are not genuinely independent. Measured above; the sequential lane metered 2.6x fewer tokens than the two-subagent fan-out, cost roughly five times less on a price-weighted basis, and was faster.

Deny the Explore fan-out entirely by adding Agent(Explore) to the deny list in ~/.claude/settings.json. The Task tool was renamed Agent in Claude Code 2.1.63, and the older Task(Explore) form readers shared still works as an alias. Cap concurrency too, so a review step cannot recruit forty-one verifiers. We did not run matched lanes for these; they gate the swarm rather than shrink it, so when they bite, the saving is the whole fan-out delta above.

And stagger cold fan-outs. The cache race above only bites when parallel spawns hit a cold prefix together, so when you do fan out wide, letting the first subagent start before the rest follow avoids paying the full prefix several times over.

OpenCode, for contrast

OpenCode's subagents carry a deliberately leaner profile. On the versions we measured, its Explore subagent requests carried a 1,485-char system prompt and 5 tools, against Claude Code's roughly 4,000 chars and 24 tools on the same Opus path. The design philosophy differs, and the per-subagent floor differs with it.

The totals stay honestly unquantified, for the second study in a row. Our matched OpenCode fan-out spawned its two Explore agents, one finished its half, the other stalled, we ended the run after fifteen minutes, and none of the subagent responses carried parseable usage through our gateway. We do not know whether the hang belongs to OpenCode's task tool, to the gateway's handling of it, or to the pairing; a raw-API run would separate them.

The dataset

As before, every captured request is written into a tamper-evident, SHA-256 hash-chained audit trail, so the numbers here are checkable rather than asserted. The chain for this session's lanes verifies at 105 entries with no breaks. The rig, the fresh subagent lanes, and the aggregated results are open source at github.com/systima-ai/agentic-coding-tools-comparison. We are also preparing the full capture set from both studies, requests, responses, and usage telemetry, as a standalone public dataset for anyone to run their own analyses on; that release gets its own post.

The takeaway

Subagents are not free parallelism. Each one is a full agent carrying its own baseline on every turn, a cold parallel spawn can re-buy that baseline outright at premium write rates, and on small tasks the fan-out is slower as well as dearer. For genuinely independent work on a big surface it buys you speed. For everything else it mostly buys you a bigger bill.

Measure it at the boundary, pin your subagents to a model you would pay for knowingly, and turn the swarm off when you do not need it.

If you run agentic systems where token cost and auditability both matter, that instrumentation is the work we do. See LLM Cost Optimisation, or read the companion piece on reducing LLM costs.

sentences.zh: 自 7 月初以来，Claude Code 的内置 Explore 代理也继承了父模型，而不是默认为 Haiku，因此，一个不被注意的扇出现在可以在您碰巧驾驶的任何昂贵模型上运行。

blocks.html_en: When we published our study of coding-agent token overhead, the loudest comment thread was about exactly this. One reader's task quietly spawned seven subagents that drained a five-hour budget, a review step tried to recruit forty-one verifiers, and later reports reached one hundred and two agents, then four hundred and fifteen. The original study held one measured fan-out already, 513,000 tokens against 121,000 for the same work done directly, its largest single multiplier. Here is the full picture, and the settings that cut the bill.

sentences.en: When we published our study of coding-agent token overhead, the loudest comment thread was about exactly this.
sentences.zh: 当我们发表对编码代理令牌开销的研究时，最响亮的评论线程正是与此相关。

sentences.en: One reader's task quietly spawned seven subagents that drained a five-hour budget, a review step tried to recruit forty-one verifiers, and later reports reached one hundred and two agents, then four hundred and fifteen.
sentences.zh: 一名读者的任务悄然催生了七个子代理，耗尽了五个小时的预算，审查步骤试图招募四十一名验证员，后来的报告到达一百零两名代理，然后是四百一十五名。

sentences.en: The original study held one measured fan-out already, 513,000 tokens against 121,000 for the same work done directly, its largest single multiplier.
sentences.zh: 最初的研究已经进行了一次测量扇出，对于直接完成的相同工作，513,000 个代币与 121,000 个代币相对，这是其最大的单一乘数。

sentences.en: Here is the full picture, and the settings that cut the bill.
sentences.zh: 这是完整的图片，以及削减费用的设置。

blocks.html_en: This is a follow-up to our harness token study. The method, the logging proxy, and the tamper-evident dataset are the same, and the rig is open source.

sentences.en: This is a follow-up to our harness token study.
sentences.zh: 这是我们的工具令牌研究的后续内容。

sentences.en: The method, the logging proxy, and the tamper-evident dataset are the same, and the rig is open source.
sentences.zh: 方法、日志代理和防篡改数据集是相同的，并且该装备是开源的。

blocks.html_en: Why subagents feel free and are not

sentences.en: Why subagents feel free and are not
sentences.zh: 为什么下属代理人感到自由，但又不自由

blocks.html_en: A subagent looks like delegation. The parent hands off a job, the subagent goes away and works, and a tidy result comes back. The parent's own context stays clean.

sentences.en: A subagent looks like delegation.
sentences.zh: 子代理看起来像委托。

sentences.en: The parent hands off a job, the subagent goes away and works, and a tidy result comes back.
sentences.zh: 父母交出一份工作，下属代理人离开去工作，然后返回一个整洁的结果。

sentences.en: The parent's own context stays clean.
sentences.zh: 父母自己的上下文保持干净。

blocks.html_en: The cost hides in three places that the tidy result does not show you.

sentences.en: The cost hides in three places that the tidy result does not show you.
sentences.zh: 成本隐藏在整洁结果无法显示的三个地方。

blocks.html_en: Each subagent is a fresh agent. It boots with its own system prompt and its own tool schemas, and it re-reads that baseline on every turn it takes, not once.

sentences.en: Each subagent is a fresh agent.
sentences.zh: 每个子代理都是新鲜代理。

sentences.en: It boots with its own system prompt and its own tool schemas, and it re-reads that baseline on every turn it takes, not once.
sentences.zh: 它以自己的系统提示符和自己的工具模式启动，并且每次都会重新读取该基线，而不是一次。

blocks.html_en: The parent can pay again on resume. While subagents run, the orchestrator's cached context can age out, and a fan-out that outlasts the cache window re-primes the parent's stack at write rates. Our lanes were short enough that the parent returned to a warm cache every time, so treat this one as mechanism rather than measurement.

sentences.en: The parent can pay again on resume.
sentences.zh: 家长可以根据简历再次付款。

sentences.en: While subagents run, the orchestrator's cached context can age out, and a fan-out that outlasts the cache window re-primes the parent's stack at write rates.
sentences.zh: 当子代理运行时，编排器的缓存上下文可能会过期，并且超出缓存窗口的扇出会以写入速率重新启动父级堆栈。

sentences.en: Our lanes were short enough that the parent returned to a warm cache every time, so treat this one as mechanism rather than measurement.
sentences.zh: 我们的通道足够短，父级每次都会返回到热缓存，因此将此视为机制而不是测量。

blocks.html_en: And the work multiplies. Two subagents running several turns each add several more full-baseline requests to the session, on top of the parent's own.

sentences.en: And the work multiplies.
sentences.zh: 而且工作量成倍增加。

sentences.en: Two subagents running several turns each add several more full-baseline requests to the session, on top of the parent's own.
sentences.zh: 运行几轮的两个子代理各自在父代理自己的请求之上向会话添加几个更多的完整基线请求。

sentences.en: What one fan-out actually cost
sentences.zh: 一个扇出的实际成本是多少

blocks.html_en: We gave Claude Code a task and let it fan out to two subagents. We ran the identical task directly for comparison. Both produced correct results.

sentences.en: We gave Claude Code a task and let it fan out to two subagents.
sentences.zh: 我们给 Claude Code 一个任务，并让它分散给两个子代理。

sentences.en: We ran the identical task directly for comparison.
sentences.zh: 我们直接运行相同的任务进行比较。

sentences.en: Both produced correct results.
sentences.zh: 两者都产生了正确的结果。

blocks.html_en: The direct run took roughly 121,000 cumulative metered input tokens. The fan-out took 513,000, a 4.2x multiplier.

sentences.en: The direct run took roughly 121,000 cumulative metered input tokens.
sentences.zh: 直接运行消耗了大约 121,000 个累计计量输入令牌。

sentences.en: The fan-out took 513,000, a 4.2x multiplier.
sentences.zh: 扇出需要 513,000 个，即 4.2 倍乘数。

blocks.html_en: The captured requests show where it went.

sentences.en: The captured requests show where it went.
sentences.zh: 捕获的请求显示了它的去向。

blocks.html_en: Every one of those five subagent requests carried its own 3,554-char system prompt and 24 of the 27 tools, and re-read a 38,000 to 69,000 token prefix. Five separate agent loops, each re-paying its own baseline on every turn, is the tax.

sentences.en: Every one of those five subagent requests carried its own 3,554-char system prompt and 24 of the 27 tools, and re-read a 38,000 to 69,000 token prefix.
sentences.zh: 这五个子代理请求中的每一个都带有自己的 3,554 个字符的系统提示符和 27 个工具中的 24 个，并重新读取 38,000 到 69,000 个令牌前缀。

sentences.en: Five separate agent loops, each re-paying its own baseline on every turn, is the tax.
sentences.zh: 五个独立的代理循环，每个循环都重新支付自己的基线，这就是税收。

sentences.en: The parent ingests results, not transcripts
sentences.zh: 家长获取结果，而不是成绩单

blocks.html_en: One thing the data settles cleanly. The parent does not swallow each subagent's entire transcript.

sentences.en: One thing the data settles cleanly.
sentences.zh: 一件事是数据干净地解决了。

sentences.en: The parent does not swallow each subagent's entire transcript.
sentences.zh: 父级不会吞下每个子代理的整个成绩单。

blocks.html_en: Across its three requests the parent's message payload grew from 8,551 to 12,490 to 15,332 characters. Those increments are the size of returned results, a few thousand characters each, not whole sub-sessions. A full subagent transcript would be tens of thousands of characters.

sentences.en: Across its three requests the parent's message payload grew from 8,551 to 12,490 to 15,332 characters.
sentences.zh: 在三个请求中，父级的消息有效负载从 8,551 个字符增加到 12,490 个字符，再增加到 15,332 个字符。

sentences.en: Those increments are the size of returned results, a few thousand characters each, not whole sub-sessions.
sentences.zh: 这些增量是返回结果的大小，每个增量是几千个字符，而不是整个子会话。

sentences.en: A full subagent transcript would be tens of thousands of characters.
sentences.zh: 完整的副特工笔录将有数万个字符。

blocks.html_en: So the multiplication is not the parent re-reading everything the subagents did. It is the subagents themselves, each a full agent in its own right, running in parallel.

sentences.en: So the multiplication is not the parent re-reading everything the subagents did.
sentences.zh: 所以乘法并不是父代理重新读取子代理所做的一切。

sentences.en: It is the subagents themselves, each a full agent in its own right, running in parallel.
sentences.zh: 它是子代理本身，每个子代理本身就是一个完整的代理，并行运行。

blocks.html_en: Are the subagent prompts cached, or re-sent each time

sentences.en: Are the subagent prompts cached, or re-sent each time
sentences.zh: 子代理提示是否已缓存或每次都重新发送

blocks.html_en: This was the sharpest disagreement in the thread. Some readers said the shared system prompt is cached and therefore cheap. Others said subagents stopped caching it.

sentences.en: This was the sharpest disagreement in the thread.
sentences.zh: 这是帖子中最尖锐的分歧。

sentences.en: Some readers said the shared system prompt is cached and therefore cheap.
sentences.zh: 一些读者表示，共享系统提示已被缓存，因此很便宜。

sentences.en: Others said subagents stopped caching it.
sentences.zh: 其他人说子代理停止缓存它。

blocks.html_en: Our captures lean towards cached, and the fresh lanes strengthen it across both new model families. In every same-model fan-out lane on Opus and on Fable, the first subagent request read 39,000 to 88,000 tokens from cache and wrote only a few hundred to roughly 6,000. That is the signature of a cache hit on the shared prefix, not a fresh write.

sentences.en: Our captures lean towards cached, and the fresh lanes strengthen it across both new model families.
sentences.zh: 我们的捕获倾向于缓存，而新的车道在两个新模型系列中都加强了它。

sentences.en: In every same-model fan-out lane on Opus and on Fable, the first subagent request read 39,000 to 88,000 tokens from cache and wrote only a few hundred to roughly 6,000.
sentences.zh: 在 Opus 和 Fable 上的每个相同型号的扇出通道中，第一个子代理请求从缓存中读取 39,000 到 88,000 个令牌，只写入几百到大约 6,000 个令牌。

sentences.en: That is the signature of a cache hit on the shared prefix, not a fresh write.
sentences.zh: 这是共享前缀上的缓存命中的签名，而不是新的写入。

blocks.html_en: The exceptions matter more, and a thread comment predicted their mechanism. Parallel spawns can race the cache; identical requests fired before the first has finished writing its prefix cannot read it, and there is no in-flight deduplication to save them.

sentences.en: The exceptions matter more, and a thread comment predicted their mechanism.
sentences.zh: 异常更重要，线程评论预测了它们的机制。

sentences.en: Parallel spawns can race the cache; identical requests fired before the first has finished writing its prefix cannot read it, and there is no in-flight deduplication to save them.
sentences.zh: 并行生成可以与缓存竞争；在第一个完成写入其前缀之前触发的相同请求无法读取它，并且没有进行中的重复数据删除来保存它们。

blocks.html_en: Our captures carry exactly that signature. Every unexpected full-prefix re-write inside a fan-out lane, meaning one where the prefix had already been written or read earlier in the lane, landed while a sibling request of the same profile was still in flight. One Haiku subagent fired six seconds after its sibling and wrote its full 52,022-token prefix with zero cache read. One Fable subagent fired twelve seconds behind two siblings that had each read the same 47,552-token prefix, and missed with a 40,829-token write. One Opus subagent turn re-wrote 56,906 tokens mid-lane with a sibling in flight. We found no unexpected re-write without concurrency.

sentences.en: Our captures carry exactly that signature.
sentences.zh: 我们的捕捉结果正是带有这样的特征。

sentences.en: Every unexpected full-prefix re-write inside a fan-out lane, meaning one where the prefix had already been written or read earlier in the lane, landed while a sibling request of the same profile was still in flight.
sentences.zh: 扇出通道内的每一次意外的全前缀重写（意味着前缀已在通道中较早写入或读取）都会在同一配置文件的同级请求仍在进行中时落地。

sentences.en: One Haiku subagent fired six seconds after its sibling and wrote its full 52,022-token prefix with zero cache read.
sentences.zh: 一个 Haiku 子代理在其同级代理后六秒触发，并以零缓存读取写入其完整的 52,022 个令牌前缀。

sentences.en: One Fable subagent fired twelve seconds behind two siblings that had each read the same 47,552-token prefix, and missed with a 40,829-token write.
sentences.zh: 一名 Fable 子代理在两个兄弟姐妹之后发射了 12 秒，这两个兄弟姐妹各自读取了相同的 47,552 个令牌前缀，但错过了 40,829 个令牌的写入。

sentences.en: One Opus subagent turn re-wrote 56,906 tokens mid-lane with a sibling in flight.
sentences.zh: 一名 Opus 副代理人在中路与一名飞行中的兄弟一起重写了 56,906 个代币。

sentences.en: We found no unexpected re-write without concurrency.
sentences.zh: 我们没有发现没有并发的意外重写。

blocks.html_en: The counterpoint appeared too. The five-subagent Opus lane fired its five spawns inside twenty seconds against a prefix warmed by an earlier lane, and every one of them read clean. So the shared prompt is cached, and a simultaneous fan-out against a cold cache is exactly where it misses, re-paying the full prefix at premium write rates each time. Caches also do not cross models, so subagents pinned to a different model maintain their own cache entry, which is how a Haiku miss can happen under an Opus parent.

sentences.en: The counterpoint appeared too.
sentences.zh: 对位也出现了。

sentences.en: The five-subagent Opus lane fired its five spawns inside twenty seconds against a prefix warmed by an earlier lane, and every one of them read clean.
sentences.zh: 五个子代理 Opus 通道在二十秒内针对由较早通道加热的前缀发射了五个生成物，并且每个都读取干净。

sentences.en: So the shared prompt is cached, and a simultaneous fan-out against a cold cache is exactly where it misses, re-paying the full prefix at premium write rates each time.
sentences.zh: 因此，共享提示会被缓存，并且针对冷缓存的同时扇出正是它错过的地方，每次都会以较高的写入速率重新支付完整的前缀。

sentences.en: Caches also do not cross models, so subagents pinned to a different model maintain their own cache entry, which is how a Haiku miss can happen under an Opus parent.
sentences.zh: 缓存也不会跨模型，因此固定到不同模型的子代理会维护自己的缓存条目，这就是在 Opus 父级下发生俳句未命中的原因。

blocks.html_en: The remaining caveat is unchanged. All of this ran through Meridian, an open-source local proxy that bridges a Claude Max or Pro subscription with standard developer environments by translating requests from external coding applications into the official format required by the Claude Agent SDK, the underlying engine for Claude Code. Meridian manages caching itself, and cache attribution is exactly the measurement a gateway is most likely to distort. The pattern is now consistent across lanes and model families, but a raw-API confirmation stays on the list before we state it as settled fact.

sentences.en: The remaining caveat is unchanged.
sentences.zh: 其余警告不变。

sentences.en: All of this ran through Meridian, an open-source local proxy that bridges a Claude Max or Pro subscription with standard developer environments by translating requests from external coding applications into the official format required by the Claude Agent SDK, the underlying engine for Claude Code.
sentences.zh: 所有这些都通过 Meridian 运行，这是一个开源本地代理，通过将外部编码应用程序的请求转换为 Claude Agent SDK（Claude Code 的底层引擎）所需的官方格式，将 Claude Max 或 Pro 订阅与标准开发人员环境连接起来。

sentences.en: Meridian manages caching itself, and cache attribution is exactly the measurement a gateway is most likely to distort.
sentences.zh: Meridian 自行管理缓存，而缓存属性正是网关最有可能扭曲的衡量标准。

sentences.en: The pattern is now consistent across lanes and model families, but a raw-API confirmation stays on the list before we state it as settled fact.
sentences.zh: 现在，该模式在各个车道和模型系列中是一致的，但在我们将其声明为既定事实之前，原始 API 确认仍保留在列表中。

sentences.en: Fan-out versus sequential, measured
sentences.zh: 扇出与顺序，测量

blocks.html_en: We ran the same small task three ways on Opus, through the same rig, each in a fresh workspace isolated from the others. All three completed the task correctly. Wall time in the tables below is the elapsed clock time from launching a lane to its final response.

sentences.en: All three completed the task correctly.

sentences.en: Wall time in the tables below is the elapsed clock time from launching a lane to its final response.
sentences.zh: 下表中的 Wall time 是从启动通道到最终响应所用的时钟时间。

blocks.html_en: Two subagents cost 2.6x the sequential run in metered tokens. Five cost 3.2x. On Sonnet in the original study the two-subagent multiplier was 4.2x, so the width of the tax varies with the model and with how many turns each subagent decides to take.

sentences.en: Two subagents cost 2.6x the sequential run in metered tokens.
sentences.zh: 两个子代理的计量令牌成本是连续运行的 2.6 倍。

sentences.en: Five cost 3.2x.
sentences.zh: 五个成本为 3.2 倍。

sentences.en: On Sonnet in the original study the two-subagent multiplier was 4.2x, so the width of the tax varies with the model and with how many turns each subagent decides to take.
sentences.zh: 在原始研究中的 Sonnet 上，两个子代理的乘数为 4.2 倍，因此税收的宽度随模型以及每个子代理决定采取的轮数而变化。

blocks.html_en: The metered totals understate the price difference. The sequential lane's tokens were mostly cache reads, billed at a tenth of the input rate. The two-subagent lane's cost was concentrated in premium-rate cache writes, including a 49,576-token write on the parent's first turn and that 56,906-token mid-lane re-write. Weight each lane by published API cache pricing and the two-subagent fan-out lands at roughly five times the sequential cost, and the five-subagent lane at roughly three times. Subscription plans meter quota rather than list prices, so the weighting is an API-rate statement, not a claim about your Max allowance.

sentences.en: The metered totals understate the price difference.
sentences.zh: 计量总数低估了价格差异。

sentences.en: The sequential lane's tokens were mostly cache reads, billed at a tenth of the input rate.
sentences.zh: 顺序通道的令牌主要是缓存读取，按输入速率的十分之一计费。

sentences.en: The two-subagent lane's cost was concentrated in premium-rate cache writes, including a 49,576-token write on the parent's first turn and that 56,906-token mid-lane re-write.
sentences.zh: 两个子代理通道的成本集中在高速率缓存写入上，包括父级第一轮的 49,576 个令牌写入和 56,906 个令牌中间通道重写。

sentences.en: Weight each lane by published API cache pricing and the two-subagent fan-out lands at roughly five times the sequential cost, and the five-subagent lane at roughly three times.
sentences.zh: 通过已发布的 API 缓存定价对每个通道进行加权，两个子代理扇出的成本约为顺序成本的五倍，五个子代理扇出的成本约为顺序成本的三倍。

sentences.en: Subscription plans meter quota rather than list prices, so the weighting is an API-rate statement, not a claim about your Max allowance.
sentences.zh: 订阅计划按计量配额而不是标价，因此权重是 API 费率声明，而不是有关最大限额的声明。

blocks.html_en: That ordering surprises people. Five subagents metered more tokens than two but cost less in weighted terms, because the two-subagent lane suffered two full prefix re-writes and the five-subagent lane did not. The mid-session re-write events the original study flagged move the bill around more than fan-out width does.

sentences.en: That ordering surprises people.
sentences.zh: 这种顺序让人们感到惊讶。

sentences.en: Five subagents metered more tokens than two but cost less in weighted terms, because the two-subagent lane suffered two full prefix re-writes and the five-subagent lane did not.
sentences.zh: 五个子代理计量的令牌比两个子代理多，但按加权计算成本较低，因为两个子代理通道经历了两次完整的前缀重写，而五个子代理通道则没有。

sentences.en: The mid-session re-write events the original study flagged move the bill around more than fan-out width does.
sentences.zh: 原始研究标记的会话中期重写事件比扇出宽度对账单的影响更大。

blocks.html_en: One fairness note. The lanes ran back to back, so the sequential lane enjoyed a warm cache on its reads. Give it a cold first write and the two-subagent multiplier drops from 2.6x to roughly 2.2x metered. Every conclusion survives the adjustment.

sentences.en: One fairness note.
sentences.zh: 一张公平的笔记。

sentences.en: The lanes ran back to back, so the sequential lane enjoyed a warm cache on its reads.
sentences.zh: 通道背靠背运行，因此顺序通道在读取时享有热缓存。

sentences.en: Give it a cold first write and the two-subagent multiplier drops from 2.6x to roughly 2.2x metered.
sentences.zh: 对其进行冷首次写入，两个子代理的乘数从 2.6 倍下降到大约 2.2 倍（按计量）。

sentences.en: Every conclusion survives the adjustment.
sentences.zh: 每个结论都经受住了调整。

Cap concurrency too, so a review step cannot recruit forty-one verifiers. We did not run matched lanes for these; they gate the swarm rather than shrink it, so when they bite, the saving is the whole fan-out delta above.

blocks.html_en: And stagger cold fan-outs. The cache race above only bites when parallel spawns hit a cold prefix together, so when you do fan out wide, letting the first subagent start before the rest follow avoids paying the full prefix several times over.

sentences.en: OpenCode, for contrast
sentences.zh: OpenCode，用于对比

blocks.html_en: OpenCode's subagents carry a deliberately leaner profile. On the versions we measured, its Explore subagent requests carried a 1,485-char system prompt and 5 tools, against Claude Code's roughly 4,000 chars and 24 tools on the same Opus path. The design philosophy differs, and the per-subagent floor differs with it.

blocks.html_en: The totals stay honestly unquantified, for the second study in a row. Our matched OpenCode fan-out spawned its two Explore agents, one finished its half, the other stalled, we ended the run after fifteen minutes, and none of the subagent responses carried parseable usage through our gateway. We do not know whether the hang belongs to OpenCode's task tool, to the gateway's handling of it, or to the pairing; a raw-API run would separate them.

blocks.html_en: As before, every captured request is written into a tamper-evident, SHA-256 hash-chained audit trail, so the numbers here are checkable rather than asserted. The chain for this session's lanes verifies at 105 entries with no breaks. The rig, the fresh subagent lanes, and the aggregated results are open source at github.com/systima-ai/agentic-coding-tools-comparison. We are also preparing the full capture set from both studies, requests, responses, and usage telemetry, as a standalone public dataset for anyone to run their own analyses on; that release gets its own post.

sentences.en: The takeaway
sentences.zh: 外卖

blocks.html_en: Subagents are not free parallelism. Each one is a full agent carrying its own baseline on every turn, a cold parallel spawn can re-buy that baseline outright at premium write rates, and on small tasks the fan-out is slower as well as dearer. For genuinely independent work on a big surface it buys you speed. For everything else it mostly buys you a bigger bill.

sentences.en: Subagents are not free parallelism.
sentences.zh: 子代理不是自由并行。

sentences.en: Each one is a full agent carrying its own baseline on every turn, a cold parallel spawn can re-buy that baseline outright at premium write rates, and on small tasks the fan-out is slower as well as dearer.

sentences.en: For genuinely independent work on a big surface it buys you speed.
sentences.zh: 对于在大面积上真正独立的工作，它可以为您带来速度。

sentences.en: For everything else it mostly buys you a bigger bill.
sentences.zh: 对于其他一切，它大多会给你带来更大的账单。

blocks.html_en: Measure it at the boundary, pin your subagents to a model you would pay for knowingly, and turn the swarm off when you do not need it.

sentences.en: Measure it at the boundary, pin your subagents to a model you would pay for knowingly, and turn the swarm off when you do not need it.
sentences.zh: 在边界处测量它，将您的子代理固定到您愿意付费的模型上，并在不需要时关闭集群。

blocks.html_en: If you run agentic systems where token cost and auditability both matter, that instrumentation is the work we do. See LLM Cost Optimisation, or read the companion piece on reducing LLM costs.

sentences.zh: 如果您运行代币成本和可审计性都很重要的代理系统，那么我们所做的工作就是检测。

sentences.zh: 请参阅 LLM 成本优化，或阅读有关降低 LLM 成本的姊妹篇。

content_html_zh:
Claude Code 的子代理使用的输入令牌数量是按顺序执行相同工作的 2.6 倍到 5.9 倍（在三个模型系列中进行测量）。 他们在我们计时的任何任务上都没有更快。 在一对匹配的缓存未命中中，价格加权成本大约是顺序运行的五倍。

第一个知道我们何时发布有关人工智能和量子治理的原创研究或思想文章。

没有垃圾邮件。 随时取消订阅。

子代理看起来像是廉价的并行性，因为父代理只接收它们的紧凑结果。 在下面，每个代理循环都是一个单独的代理循环，每轮都携带自己的系统提示符、工具和缓存条目。

自 7 月初以来，Claude Code 的内置 Explore 代理也继承了父模型，而不是默认为 Haiku，因此，一个不被注意的扇出现在可以在您碰巧驾驶的任何昂贵模型上运行。

当我们发表对编码代理令牌开销的研究时，最响亮的评论线程正是与此相关。 一名读者的任务悄然催生了七个子代理，耗尽了五个小时的预算，审查步骤试图招募四十一名验证员，后来的报告到达一百零两名代理，然后是四百一十五名。 最初的研究已经进行了一次测量扇出，对于直接完成的相同工作，513,000 个代币与 121,000 个代币相对，这是其最大的单一乘数。 这是完整的图片，以及削减费用的设置。

这是我们的工具令牌研究的后续内容。 方法、日志代理和防篡改数据集是相同的，并且该装备是开源的。

为什么下属代理人感到自由，但又不自由

子代理看起来像委托。 父母交出一份工作，下属代理人离开去工作，然后返回一个整洁的结果。 父母自己的上下文保持干净。

成本隐藏在整洁结果无法显示的三个地方。

每个子代理都是新鲜代理。 它以自己的系统提示符和自己的工具模式启动，并且每次都会重新读取该基线，而不是一次。

家长可以根据简历再次付款。 当子代理运行时，编排器的缓存上下文可能会过期，并且超出缓存窗口的扇出会以写入速率重新启动父级堆栈。 我们的通道足够短，父级每次都会返回到热缓存，因此将此视为机制而不是测量。

而且工作量成倍增加。 运行几轮的两个子代理各自在父代理自己的请求之上向会话添加几个更多的完整基线请求。

一个扇出的实际成本是多少

我们给 Claude Code 一个任务，并让它分散给两个子代理。 我们直接运行相同的任务进行比较。 两者都产生了正确的结果。

直接运行消耗了大约 121,000 个累计计量输入令牌。 扇出需要 513,000 个，即 4.2 倍乘数。

家长获取结果，而不是成绩单

一件事是数据干净地解决了。 父级不会吞下每个子代理的整个成绩单。

在三个请求中，父级的消息有效负载从 8,551 个字符增加到 12,490 个字符，再增加到 15,332 个字符。 这些增量是返回结果的大小，每个增量是几千个字符，而不是整个子会话。 完整的副特工笔录将有数万个字符。

所以乘法并不是父代理重新读取子代理所做的一切。 它是子代理本身，每个子代理本身就是一个完整的代理，并行运行。

子代理提示是否已缓存或每次都重新发送

这是帖子中最尖锐的分歧。 一些读者表示，共享系统提示已被缓存，因此很便宜。 其他人说子代理停止缓存它。

我们的捕获倾向于缓存，而新的车道在两个新模型系列中都加强了它。 在 Opus 和 Fable 上的每个相同型号的扇出通道中，第一个子代理请求从缓存中读取 39,000 到 88,000 个令牌，只写入几百到大约 6,000 个令牌。 这是共享前缀上的缓存命中的签名，而不是新的写入。

异常更重要，线程评论预测了它们的机制。 并行生成可以与缓存竞争；在第一个完成写入其前缀之前触发的相同请求无法读取它，并且没有进行中的重复数据删除来保存它们。

我们的捕捉结果正是带有这样的特征。 扇出通道内的每一次意外的全前缀重写（意味着前缀已在通道中较早写入或读取）都会在同一配置文件的同级请求仍在进行中时落地。 一个 Haiku 子代理在其同级代理后六秒触发，并以零缓存读取写入其完整的 52,022 个令牌前缀。 一名 Fable 子代理在两个兄弟姐妹之后发射了 12 秒，这两个兄弟姐妹各自读取了相同的 47,552 个令牌前缀，但错过了 40,829 个令牌的写入。 一名 Opus 副代理人在中路与一名飞行中的兄弟一起重写了 56,906 个代币。 我们没有发现没有并发的意外重写。

对位也出现了。 五个子代理 Opus 通道在二十秒内针对由较早通道加热的前缀发射了五个生成物，并且每个都读取干净。 因此，共享提示会被缓存，并且针对冷缓存的同时扇出正是它错过的地方，每次都会以较高的写入速率重新支付完整的前缀。 缓存也不会跨模型，因此固定到不同模型的子代理会维护自己的缓存条目，这就是在 Opus 父级下发生俳句未命中的原因。

其余警告不变。 所有这些都通过 Meridian 运行，这是一个开源本地代理，通过将外部编码应用程序的请求转换为 Claude Agent SDK（Claude Code 的底层引擎）所需的官方格式，将 Claude Max 或 Pro 订阅与标准开发人员环境连接起来。 Meridian 自行管理缓存，而缓存属性正是网关最有可能扭曲的衡量标准。 现在，该模式在各个车道和模型系列中是一致的，但在我们将其声明为既定事实之前，原始 API 确认仍保留在列表中。

两个子代理的计量令牌成本是连续运行的 2.6 倍。 五个成本为 3.2 倍。 在原始研究中的 Sonnet 上，两个子代理的乘数为 4.2 倍，因此税收的宽度随模型以及每个子代理决定采取的轮数而变化。

计量总数低估了价格差异。 顺序通道的令牌主要是缓存读取，按输入速率的十分之一计费。 两个子代理通道的成本集中在高速率缓存写入上，包括父级第一轮的 49,576 个令牌写入和 56,906 个令牌中间通道重写。 通过已发布的 API 缓存定价对每个通道进行加权，两个子代理扇出的成本约为顺序成本的五倍，五个子代理扇出的成本约为顺序成本的三倍。 订阅计划按计量配额而不是标价，因此权重是 API 费率声明，而不是有关最大限额的声明。

这种顺序让人们感到惊讶。 五个子代理计量的令牌比两个子代理多，但按加权计算成本较低，因为两个子代理通道经历了两次完整的前缀重写，而五个子代理通道则没有。 原始研究标记的会话中期重写事件比扇出宽度对账单的影响更大。

一张公平的笔记。 通道背靠背运行，因此顺序通道在读取时享有热缓存。 对其进行冷首次写入，两个子代理的乘数从 2.6 倍下降到大约 2.2 倍（按计量）。 每个结论都经受住了调整。

而关于速度的争论从未出现过。 两个副特工通道需要八分钟，而顺序通道需要四又四分之一，甚至五个并行副特工也只能与顺序速度相匹配。 对于这么小的任务，编排开销吞没了整个并行性。

模型继承陷阱

自 2026 年 7 月 1 日发布的 Claude Code 2.1.198 以来，内置 Explore 代理继承了主会话的模型，上限为 Opus，而不是像以前那样在 Haiku 上运行。 （变更日志）

这是一个悄然发生的成本变化。 过去用于旋转廉价俳句探险家的扇出现在可以旋转您驾驶的任何昂贵模型的副本，除非您另有说明。

我们衡量了其他说法的价值。 同样的两个子代理扇出运行一次与继承的 Opus 子代理一起运行，一次与固定到 Haiku 的子代理一起运行，而父代理留在 Opus 上。

固定通道计量的输入代币减少了 37%，完成时间不到一半，并将大约 150,000 个剩余代币转移到 Haiku 的价格单上，这只是 Opus 费率的一小部分。 Haiku 子代理也完成了 3 个请求，而 Opus 子代理则完成了 8 个请求，这也是它自己的节省。

出现了一项新成本。 缓存不跨模型，因此 Haiku 子代理维护自己的缓存条目，并且我们的子代理之一以零缓存读取重写了其完整的 52,022 个令牌前缀。 在一次性扇出时，写入会消耗节省的资金；在重复扇出的会话中它会摊销。

这是一对匹配的，而不是分布。 但方向是明确的，并且它为上面的变更日志变化定价。 继承 Opus 而不是在 Haiku 上运行的 Explore 群每次触发时的成本大致相当于这两行之间的差值。

如何减税

该线程提供了缓解措施。 这是我们可以添加数字的内容。

将子代理固定到更便宜的模型而不是继承驱动程序。 以上测量；计量令牌减少了 37%，占用时间减少了一半，并且分代理流量按 Haiku 费率计费。

当子任务并非真正独立时，更喜欢顺序工作。 以上测量；顺序通道计量的令牌比两个子代理扇出少 2.6 倍，按价格加权计算成本大约低五倍，而且速度更快。

通过将 Agent(Explore) 添加到 ~/.claude/settings.json 中的拒绝列表来完全拒绝 Explore 扇出。 任务工具在 Claude Code 2.1.63 中被重命名为 Agent，旧的任务（探索）表单阅读器共享仍然作为别名使用。 并发性也受到限制，因此审查步骤无法招募 41 个验证者。 我们没有为这些运行匹配的车道；它们对群体进行门控而不是收缩，因此当它们咬住时，节省的就是上面的整个扇出增量。

并错开冷扇出。 上面的缓存竞争仅在并行生成同时命中冷前缀时起作用，因此当您确实广泛扇出时，让第一个子代理在其余子代理之前启动可以避免多次支付完整前缀。

OpenCode，用于对比

OpenCode 的子代理具有特意精简的配置。 在我们测量的版本中，其 Explore 子代理请求带有 1,485 个字符的系统提示和 5 个工具，而 Claude Code 在同一 Opus 路径上大约有 4,000 个字符和 24 个工具。 设计理念不同，每个子代理的楼层也不同。

老实说，这是连续第二项研究，总数仍然没有量化。 我们匹配的 OpenCode 扇出产生了两个探索代理，一个完成了一半，另一个停止了，我们在十五分钟后结束了运行，并且没有一个子代理响应通过我们的网关携带可解析的使用情况。 我们不知道该挂起是否属于 OpenCode 的任务工具、网关对其的处理或配对；原始 API 运行会将它们分开。

数据集

与以前一样，每个捕获的请求都会写入防篡改的 SHA-256 哈希链审计跟踪中，因此此处的数字是可检查的而不是断言的。 此会话通道的链验证了 105 个条目，没有中断。 该装备、新的子代理通道和聚合结果均在 github.com/systima-ai/agentic-coding-tools-comparison 上开源。 我们还准备了来自研究、请求、响应和使用遥测的完整捕获集，作为独立的公共数据集，供任何人运行自己的分析；该版本有自己的帖子。

外卖

子代理不是自由并行。 每个都是一个完整的代理，每回合都携带自己的基线，冷并行生成可以以高写入速率完全重新购买该基线，并且在小任务上，扇出更慢也更昂贵。 对于在大面积上真正独立的工作，它可以为您带来速度。 对于其他一切，它大多会给你带来更大的账单。

在边界处测量它，将您的子代理固定到您愿意付费的模型上，并在不需要时关闭集群。

如果您运行代币成本和可审计性都很重要的代理系统，那么我们所做的工作就是检测。 请参阅 LLM 成本优化，或阅读有关降低 LLM 成本的姊妹篇。
